hotel_name: Fairmont Resort & Spa Blue Mountains - MGallery by Sofitel

reviews.title: No worth it AT ALL
reviews.text:
Swing pool was dysfunctional
Room services were not delivered.
Such a chaos in this resort.
Food was not good
reviews.room_type: Deluxe King Room with Spa Bath

reviews.title: Absolutely rubbish and not worth $500 stay nor carry the Fairmont standard.
reviews.negatives:
- Staff are mostly new (maybe even casual?), and not trained well.  They just respond with strict fact without offering any helpful suggestion. 
- It's quite obvious they are under staff and don't get anything done. Eg., asking for towel or toiletries took 6 hours to get delivered. 
- Parking is hard/difficult
- Facilities are old and not maintained properly.

reviews.title: Unauthorized charges
reviews.text: Unreasonably charge to my credit card without informing me, claiming they cannot get it sorted out with agoda and just charging unknown charges to me. This happened more than 1.5 months after i checked out and had paid fully for the rooms back then. Extremely bad for such a known hotel.
reviews.response:
Dear Khim
We believe this has now been rectified and apologise for any confusion,
The Fairmont Resort & Spa Team

reviews.title: I would not recommend to anyone to stay there
reviews.positives: The view and location
reviews.negatives: The service as there was no customer focus at all

reviews.title: Unknown charges after more than 1 month
reviews.text: Suddenly received an unposted charge on my credit card after more than 1 month of staying, where i have already paid for it long ago.

reviews.title: Not Romantic, Not Luxury. Kids only.
reviews.positives: IF we had kids, maybe this would be a place to keep them busy.  They had indoor ice rink, pony rides, face painting, rock painting, mini train ride if weather is good.
reviews.negatives: It was kind of unclean, gross.  There was room service left outside of rooms for nearly 24 hours.  The view is almost not existent.  There is a colorful 100+ yards kids slide in the Mountain View.  Notice that all the resort pictures are from the sky and above.  Don’t be fooled.  This is not a romantic or luxury place.  The breakfast buffet was like a kids daycare.  The tennis courts are carpet and not maintained.  If it rains, it would be days before the tennis carpet dries.  There was hardly any water available, the water containers were never filled.  He menus were very sticky I guess from the kids.

reviews.title: Too expensive and you get nothing
reviews.text:
They claim that they offer good break for family with kids but we you get there 
1. Too many residents and too little staff so nothing get done on time
2. Kids activities,  either not working or poorly run. And on top of the very expensive cost per night you still have to pay for every single kids activities,  that if anything worse to be done
3. Rooms and facilities are 200 years old 

2nd time there,  but never again

reviews.title: Overpriced and disappointing, unclean dining areas, unsuitable for anyone except large families
reviews.positives: Comfortable room
reviews.negatives:
The price we paid was far too high for the quality of the facilities and services provided. 
I would not recommend booking here unless you are a large family, as the experience did not meet our expectations. 
We specifically chose this hotel believing that all amenities would be conveniently on-site, but were disappointed to find that the spa closed early at 5pm and one bar was permanently shut. 
The pool area was overcrowded with children, making it quite uninviting. 
Finally, breakfast was a very unpleasant experience - the dining area was extremely dirty, with food scattered on the floor and uncleaned tables. 
Overall, I was dissatisfied with both the service and the overall value for money.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Our room was dirty when we arrived, including poo stains in the toilet and used tissues on the bedside table, additionally our tv wasn’t working and it took them 2 days to fix. A number of the activities were out of order and they were understaffed for how busy it is during the school holidays, to be fair the staff that were there were pleasant and hard working.

reviews.title: Not worth for the money.
reviews.positives: Great location
reviews.negatives:
Facilities are old, and breakfast is very limited 
We requested to add a sofa bed and had to call customer service to ask for bed sheet etc.

reviews.title: Cleanliness pending improvement
reviews.text: Feels a bit odd on the Receptionists, cleanliness of room pending improvement
reviews.response:
Dear Fung,

Thank you for taking the time to send through feedback on your recent stay. I am sorry that we did not meet your expectations. 

We will certainly take this feedback on board and thank you for taking the time to send this through.

Kind Regards,

Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains

reviews.title: poor to average, not worth the cost
reviews.positives: Room was adequate
reviews.negatives: The carpet in room was very poor and in need of repair

reviews.title: One word: run
reviews.text: It's  great location with great views but that's it. Really old property and we had bed bugs plus a leaky roof
reviews.response:
Dear Tara,

Thank you for taking the time to send through feedback on your recent stay. I am sorry that we did not meet your expectations. 

We will certainly take this feedback on board and thank you for taking the time to send this through. As we had discussed with you at the time, we were unable to locate any bed bugs in the room you occupied, but thank you again for the feedback.

Kind Regards,

Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains

reviews.title: Big property but outdated facilities
reviews.positives: Liked the location of hotel which is 5 min drive from leura village. Indoor heated swimming pool and buffet breakfast was decent with mesmerizing view of mountains. Bed and pillow was comfortable and clean bathroom. Staffs in the breakfast, concierge, housekeeping were really warm and greeted when seen.
reviews.negatives:
Facilities in the entire hotel was extremely outdated and when I say it, you will see many areas barricaded due to out of use, chipped walls/tiles in most places so doesnt give you the resort kind of ambience and wasnt value for the money we paid. 

All the Game rooms for kids had a very damp smell, looked like wasnt managed well, wasnt well ventilated so we didnt spend any time there even though my son wanted to due to the smell. The TV in our room had    Option of buying movies and it was accidentally subscribed by my kid as it was just a click to subscribe it, no card payment nothing required and when told the same during checkout, we still had to pay 35 dollars for the accident. Front office staff didnt seem really welcoming during checkin or checkout. They could do more when welcoming guests and bidding goodbye as they are the face of hotel and not once the staff asked us how the stay was or anything which was very unusual. The hotel certainly wont be on my list to come back, a bit disappointing.

reviews.title: Booking mixed up
reviews.text: I booked 2 nights for 2 rooms. I also called the hotel to ensure these 2 rooms are adjacent to each other and they have arranged for that. When I checked in, the other room was booked for a wrong date in their system so I had to wait for a long time before we can settle in the room. The room was also not clean, there were left over ice cream in the freezer, missing teaspoon for coffee, incomplete tea. The next day, the hotel key did not work as their system is again reflecting wrong date.

reviews.title: Disappointing
reviews.positives: Great facilities for the kids
reviews.negatives:
There were bed bugs. My kids and I shared a king size bed and our bodies were all covered in raised red lumps by the end of the trip. I raised this with reception and was kindly given zyrtec and immediately told a room change was not possible over my 3 night stay as rooms were all booked out. 
I also gave notice of needing a shower chair for my disabled daughter and was assured there'll be one organised upon my arrival, there wasn't one in the bathroom. I called reception and they said they'll bring one straight away only to receive a call a few minutes informing me the shower chair isn't available, a step was provided instead, clearly not a substitute for someone who cannot stand. I then requested for a small stool I could place inside the bathtub, was told he'd go and check and then never heard back again. I didn't bother calling back.
The bathtub is also extremely slippery and a rubber mat was also not available upon asking causing a slip accident and bruised leg.

reviews.title: A glorified and unsupervised kindergarten
reviews.text:
the check in which was an hour late at 4.00pm
In house dining where there is no table in the room and only one chair. 
Very limited menu in the restaurant 
Breakfast is like an unsupervised kindergarten where screaming kids run a mom , the room is filthy with food scraps all over the floor and tables left dirty. 
Concierge when approached for guidance hands out brochures and tells you to call the operator directly or book on line. Absolutely no assistance or advice 
The rooms are barely cleaned with no attention to detail. The spa was left with soap leftover from previous evening. 
Parking is available but need to walk over 5 minutes to get to reception without any cover on rainy day. 
Umbrella's not provided

Overall the most disappointing and frustrating hotel experience. 

Will never ever come back again
reviews.room_type: Deluxe King Room with Spa Bath

reviews.title: A total let down despite the hard working staff.
reviews.positives: The location and views of the mountains are beautiful. The staff are excellent
reviews.negatives: The property is old and tired and desperately needs refurbishment. The rooms are poor and the beds are utterly unacceptable. Criminal to charge people to sleep in these beds. The property is bizarre in its opening and closing times with many venues closed completely or closing very early.  Room service menu is a joke and not available after 9pm.  Potential here is huge but let down by Accor and hotel management despite the bar and wait staff being awesome and doing their best.

reviews.title: Expensive and very poor service
reviews.text:
We stayed there for one night and were really disappointed. Check in at 9pm, first of all, you need to unload the luggage anbd no one is there to help, and there is a long corridor from the road to the hallway... No concierge available so you really have to do all by yourself.
When you see the standing of the hotel it isnt very normal.
Second we asked for a babycot that was never brought to the room.
At 9.30pm we couldnt eat anywhere except for the room service, where you could only order a very small pizza for a very expensive amount! We were very disppointed by the poor service. One person at the check in only, no one to help with luggage when check in/out...
Not very convenient when you are with kids and only have 2 hands
Will not go back again

reviews.title: Poor hospitality, dated hotel, amazing location
reviews.positives: The location is spectacular
reviews.negatives:
We stayed during school holidays and had just road tripped from Victoria. All we wanted was a nice meal and a good sleep. When we arrived the restaurant was booked out and we were told only room service was available. The bar/lounge stops serving food at 5pm. The room service menu looked ok - but then when we tried to place an order nobody would pick up the phone. My wife walked to the front desk (who also wouldn’t pick up the phone) only to be told that room service was pizza only! Three other guests were at the front desk going through the same experience. We were told we could drive into the local town!!!

The whole hospitality experience was understaffed and not befitting of a hotel or resort experience.

reviews.title: Disappointing, hallway had old carpets. not up to
reviews.text: Disappointing, hotel was not up to expection. Was told by hotel staff they re not affiliated with Fairmonts hotel in Canad. Not worth the price for a resort hotel.
reviews.positives: Big lobby, location

reviews.title: Definitely not value for money. A fallen star.
reviews.positives: Sadly, very little. I had been there a few years ago for a coffee and thought it was stunning. All that beauty was lost due to a myriad of issues. I can say the shower was hot, so that was good.
reviews.negatives: Right from the beginning, the service was poor. Nobody available to help with bags, a thousand children running wild around the foyer, the Airconditioning was freezing, the paint was chipped all along the skirtings, the distant views were wonderful, but looked over dry grass with no landscaping and patches of dirt, the breakfast room has glass windows to take in the ‘view’ that have a massive solid metal bar across the middle of the glass which blocks the view. We waited 40 minutes for a coffee and a cup of tea at the cafe, had to clear coffee cups from the pool table to play a game… all in all, a big disappointment.

reviews.title: Under the pump
reviews.text:
Great facilities, fun activities and location
But we found used spoons in the room, not washed..no soap dispenser and we had to call 3 times to get a dental kit..overall don’t feel five star at all. The used spoons were definitely a put off

reviews.title: Disappointing to see such a lovely property so run down. The staff should be commended for their commitment to customer
reviews.positives: Location & staff so helpful.
reviews.negatives: Run down, needs painting, pool looked dirty & maintenance overall of property obviously not happened. Holes in room curtains, paint peeling on all surfaces outside. One gate to pool had hoses running across it which dangerous. Signage to facilitates inadequate. Food almost inedible from room service, ambience in Embers restaurant lacking.

reviews.title: Need update
reviews.text: We went there for family short trip (we are 5 of us with 8 yo, 5yo, 2yo). with another young family. I called hotel to ask about something, but the staff on the phone was not helpful and friendly either. All the other staff in the hotel were generally not friendly except concierge and a staff for kids ride. The room was clean but bits and pieces were old. And needed updated like shower. Switch button for tap and shower wasn’t work, had to pull the button all the time to fill up bath. We saw rubbish on corridors and people’s used towels all the time. Asked a cleaner to change towels only, but another cleaner came and made bed. Most of activities for kids costed extra cost. Not enough explanation for activities we could do in the hotel. Pool towels were not available for long time, we had to wait for them to come. The breakfast wasn’t impressive experience compared to the price we paid. All over, little bit disappointed experience.
reviews.response:
Dear Eiko,

Thank you for sharing your feedback about your recent stay. We apologize for the inconvenience and disappointment you experienced during your visit.

It's concerning to hear about the unfriendly service you encountered from some of our staff members. Providing exceptional customer service is a top priority for us, and your comments will be addressed with our team to ensure improvements are made.

We are pleased to know that you found the room clean, but we regret that certain amenities, such as the shower, did not meet your expectations. Maintenance issues like the faulty switch button should not have occurred, and we will ensure this is promptly fixed.

We appreciate your feedback and hope to have the opportunity to provide you with a more enjoyable and memorable stay in the future. Thank you for bringing these matters to our attention.

Kind Regards,

Rory Douglas
Executive Assistant Manager

reviews.title: Room furniture is dated , don’t even provide basic stuff like water , slippers . Tooth brush . When asked for it some ne
reviews.positives: Location is okay
reviews.negatives: Old in need of renovation

reviews.title: This is a motel disguised as a resort
reviews.text: If you are used to resorts in Asia, this place is a motel by those standards. They have most of.the things shuton weekends (pony ride, train ride, half the restaurants) and even their breakfast buffet is more that of a  star hotel (self service literally and barely any vegan options). Staff is friendly but this is really as bare bones as a resort can get. The indoor heated pool was barely warm, thought my kids caught cold from there. The room service and housekeeping never pick up the phone, had to request for extra towels and slippers and dental kits (when its a room for 4, wonder why they skimp on basics like these). Only saving grace is hop on hop off bus has a stop here. Recommend staying in katoomba or some place which is closer to the town centre.
reviews.response:
Dear Atul,

Thank you for taking the time to send through feedback on your recent stay. I am sorry that we did not meet your expectations. 

We will certainly take this feedback on board and thank you for taking the time to send this through.

Kind Regards,

Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: Nothing was open if we knew so many resort activities where closed would have booked elsewhere for less

reviews.title: Good for families
reviews.text:
The Location isn’t near anything so you need a car.
The majority of facilities are child / family focussed (not us). The reception staff didn't have a clue about what was on the property. Even though they checked with a supervisor, they  still misdirected us when we asked where to walk or to view sunset.  

Breakfast which was a major reason we booked here was thought by all to be a shambles   We were all complaining as we  waited to swoop if  something delivered &.the Teensy thimble of  avocado scraped empty for 2 days all morning was a joke & not replenished.

There was not a thing gourmet (Scones were as hard as a rock. 2 star was not what we paid for. Stay elsewhere and visit real local providers  who are highly recommended.   Especially the pies!
We had to sit far away in an area we ere first told e couldn’t sit to get away from the screaming out of control children
reviews.response:
Dear Leeana,

Thank you for taking the time to send through feedback on your recent stay. I am sorry that we did not meet your expectations. 

We will certainly take this feedback on board and thank you for taking the time to send this through.

Kind Regards,

Fairmont Resort Blue Mountains

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Restaurant options were good. Spa was nice.
reviews.negatives: Dirty carpets. Stains on couch. Wall paint chipped.

reviews.title: Too far out!
reviews.text:
Old, dirty carpet and couch.
Thin walls.
Very expensive for very little but not much choice in the area.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: The facilities for the kids
reviews.negatives:
The rooms were filthy and dated 
The hotel is dated and dirty 
The staff are miserable

reviews.title: Filthy
reviews.text: The resort was absolutely filthy. No cleaning in any area

reviews.title: Would not go back
reviews.positives: The breakfast was OK
reviews.negatives: No follow up from staff on requests. Felt like there was no level of interest in customer service.

reviews.title: Very disappointed!
reviews.text:
Booked for Christmas in July but we’re told that it was booked out. We ate in The other restaurant that was very expensive a not worth a cent! The scallops were hard to find through piles of crumb and were well over cooked and tasteless. The two king prawns had virtually no meat left as they too were overcooked and Were not deveined.
The room was clean but the toilet seat was all scratched up and didn’t flush properly and the shower head wouldn’t stay up.
Two nights with no sleep from people next door partying on the balcony outside our window and the constant shouting from people in the hall.
I must compliment the staff though! They were polite and helpful but that isn’t enough to make us ever go back.

reviews.title: Noisy.
reviews.positives: Breakfast buffet very comprehensive
reviews.negatives: The facility is completely geared towards families and children. Nearly no accommodation is made for adults without children. The place is overrun with children running, screaming and making mess. The common areas, restaurants, breakfast etc are unbearably loud, crowded and messy courtesy of kids and families who expect the facility to accommodate their brood. There are few places for adults to go to have quiet drink and meal or watch sport, without the carnage of hordes of children.

reviews.title: Noisy and bad room
reviews.text: Could hear all the noise from the room next door and all corridor noise. our heating wasnt working and engineer came twice. couldnt move room as they were booked out. had to get a oil heater put in our room. the shower head was broken. had to get a new one installed. Breakfast buffet really not up to scratch, nothing tasted good and the poached eggs and omelettes werent cooked right. i wouldnt stay here again.

reviews.title: pleasant but old and run down
reviews.negatives:
no restaurants are open during the week and the only few that are you have to book in advance the food
isn’t the greatest

reviews.title: Very rude reception staff
reviews.text:
The overall condition of the hotel is acceptable. However, there were instances of cleanliness issues, particularly within the room. It seems the hotel may be understaffed for effectively cleaning such a large property.
A significant drawback was the unhelpful attitude of the reception staff. He was dismissive of my inquiries and appeared rushed. I felt that my concerns were not adequately addressed. The guy who is in charge of our luggage was lovely though.
reviews.response:
Dear Evia,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re truly sorry to hear about your experience at check-in, and we sincerely apologize for the unhelpful attitude you encountered from the reception staff. This is certainly not the standard we strive for, and we will address this matter with our team to ensure that all of our guests are treated with the respect and attentiveness they deserve.

We also appreciate you bringing the cleanliness issues to our attention. We take these concerns seriously, and we will review our staffing levels and cleaning procedures to ensure that all areas of the hotel meet the high standards we aim to maintain.

On a positive note, we're glad to hear that the luggage team provided you with a warm and helpful service. We’ll make sure to pass on your kind words to them.

Thank you again for your feedback. We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back in the future and provide you with a much more seamless and pleasant experience.

Warm

reviews.title: The resort has great potential, but it falls short of its promises.
reviews.positives: I enjoyed the property’s location. The room was clean and the bed was comfortable. The breakfast was delicious and the views were great.
reviews.negatives: The heated pool was freezing, the sauna only reached 60 degrees, and the jacuzzi (spa) was cold. There was an unpleasant, pungent smell throughout the hotel. The food at the restaurants was mediocre, except for the waffle chips and breakfast. The hotel staff was unresponsive when I called them.

reviews.title: Fairmount Resorts  blue mountains
reviews.text:
There’s not much options I’m menu
With the in room dining, restaurant is quite expensive rooms are a bit dated  carpets and furniture .
Room rates expensive for what you get 
Carpet has stain , mirrors has marks it was not cleaned properly.
reviews.response:
Dear Paul

Thank you for taking the time to tell us about your experience at the Fairmont Resort & Spa Blue Mountains MGallery by Sofitel. 

I am sorry  to read that some of experience did not meet your expectations; please allow me to express my sincerest apologies. We do pride ourselves on providing only the highest quality of product and service which we appear to have fallen down on on this occasion.  Please rest assured that we are taking the appropriate measures to address the problems outlined in order to  prevent future occurrence. 

We appreciate you sharing your concerns, and it is our hope that you will give us the opportunity to better serve you in the near future. 

Sincerely,

Chamarie Rambukkana
Assistant Manager

reviews.title: Fair value for school holiday period
reviews.positives: Lots of kids activities on during school holidays, although the train was out of action for repairs.
reviews.negatives: The room description for standard rooms does not explicitly state that the shower is over a bathtub. This was not suitable for elderly or people with mobility issues. There were often no towels available at the swimming pool. There were limited menu options at the lounge bar, room service or family-friendly restaurant; mostly pizza, pasta or deep-fried options. The air-conditioning did not seem to be operating in the lounge bar on a 36 degree day.

reviews.title: I’d say go stay near the town and not in resort…
reviews.text: I’d rather stay near the bay and there’s nothing to do here… not worth a stay…
reviews.response:
Dear Angel

Thank you for taking the time to provide feedback regarding your stay at Fairmont Resort & Spa. We apologise that you felt disappointed with your stay on this occasion and found what was on offer here at the resort did not suit your tastes. We hope that we will have the opportunity in the future to welcome you back when you visit the Blue Mountains next time.

Best Regards

Chamarie Rambukkana
Assistant Manager

reviews.title: Cool and fun and strange location. Stuck in 1998 like a time capsule
reviews.positives: Cool location big building with fun and strange activities
reviews.negatives: Dirty pool, no towels available in pool area at 5pm, outdoor gym was a joke, — more importantly Aircon didn’t work in the room all that well. Thankfully staff brought us a fan but still.

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text:
Room service was below average. 
Dead bugs everywhere for my son to chew , would explain why he didn't eat at the restaurant.  Overall cleanliness was poor

reviews.title: staff were friendly but was very dissapointed didnt get room i originally booked
reviews.positives:
staff at embers was great all though they mixed our meals up with another table 
cafe was quick with food and cocktails 
was nice having king towels in bathroom.
reviews.negatives:
booking,com double booked our room! had to give us a smaller room without a balcony 
they were meant to leave a bottle of wine in our room never received it even after contacting reception.
there were no coffee cups left in room for us to have coffee in morning.
pillows to soft old pillows

reviews.title: Dont bother with this, rather book oher quaint cottages for cheaper and more character
reviews.text:
The Good: Location and the nice view you. Breakfast spread was great with lots of choices and delicious. 
The Meh: Service was hit and miss - the porter was helpful and polite but other staff were indifferent and cold and very rude on the phone. Reception insisted on checking in at 3pm when we arrived at about 230pm.
The Horrible: Long strands of hair found on bed when we checked in. Bathroom water has weak pressure. Another long hair found on towel while drying myself. Yucks! I really wonder if they bother to clean the room. Very disappointing cleanliness for a five star resort. No  complimentary water. Really awful since we just came from a fabuous five star hotel from Sydney. Dont bother with this hotel which seems to be cruising by only on the brand name and its location. I suggest booking any other accomodation here with more character and for a cheaper price. Not value for money at all.
reviews.response:
Dear Travis
Thank you for your review and feebdack, we appreciate hearing from all of our guests.
I would like to apologise to you sincerely for a failed stay with us, certainly far from our want or intention at any time and very disappointing to read. We pride ourselves on our high level of warm and personalised service and I am so sorry this did not shine through during your stay. Your comments have been reviewed with our Front Office Manager as well as our House Keeping Manager to ensure their teams are made aware.
Thank you for sharing your concerns once again,
Kind regards
Scott O'Neile
General Manager

reviews.title: Not good than before
reviews.positives: Enviroment is good, and staff is nice.
reviews.negatives: It supposed to be a kids-friendly place, but the swimming pool did not have any kids area; kids' trains are not available on Monday/ Weekdays; Food is not good, buffle breakfast has very less options. Facilities are super old. I came here 1 or 2 years ago, where the kids will get a duck toy, but not this time. I feel a little bit disappointed.

reviews.title: Disappointing
reviews.text:
Arriving after 4 pm and there was no porter insight to help with the luggage. We have to struggle oursleves with the luggages . How could this happen to a 5-star hotel?

After we got the key and go to our non-smoking room,  we smelt cigarette smokes covered by aerosol spray. We called the front desk and was told someone will come and take a look but nobody came. I have to go to front desk to get a change of room.

While in the room, we were disappointed to feel that the attention to details are just not there:
1. The faucet has a lever that is longer than the spout, it will poke your face when you try to wash your mouth.
2. A rack is placed very close to the basin that your elbow constantly hit on it. 
3. The shower curtain is too short that soap water gets splashed out all over the floor. 
4. The shower head handle is worn out that there are sharp edges protruding that will cut your hand. 
5. One of the pillowcases looks like it has been used before. It is crumpled with dirt inside.

reviews.title: It was a nice place - but expected better for the price we've paid.
reviews.positives: The resort is secluded, away from the main town.
reviews.negatives: Not enough quiet places. Arcades are old. Hard to navigate around the resort with a lack of signage, especially from car parks further away.

reviews.title: Oldish
reviews.text: Alright but too expensive for the quality of the infrastructure
reviews.positives: Personnel, huge, view

reviews.title: I will most likely choose somewhere else next time we come to the mountains.
reviews.positives: The location of the Fairmont is fabulous and the views are great.
reviews.negatives:
The 2 days we were there was overrun with children. They were in the bar area, in the expensive dining room and running around the hotel. It was overwhelming and made our stay very disappointing. It's not that the children were there, but that they were noisy and running around, and the parents did nothing to keep them quiet and resect the other guests. For the amount we paid ($353 per night for each room) our stay was spoiled with noisy children.
In addition, our breakfast wait staff not only started clearing dirty plates while I was eating, but she also reached between us to retrieve dirty cups and plates - and not just once!  Basic manners at all times please!

reviews.title: Overcrowded and costly
reviews.text: Getting overcrowded now and expensive for a room for what it is. Been a long time since an upgrade

reviews.title: It’s okay- expensive for what it is
reviews.positives: There’s no food available anywhere at night and it is not close enough to go into town. Staff who checked us in was ‘not happy to be there’- soured our first impression. Signs were up saying places were inaccessible due to events but events looked to be long over? We were unsure if we could access and did not want to bother front desk again. Pillows were cheap.

reviews.title: More Appropruate For Family With Younger children.
reviews.text: Hotel a bit run down.  Breakfast variety can be better. Thanks.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: Quality of rooms. Loose shelves. Not clean (toenails on floor!)

reviews.title: Good property, staff could be a bit more friendly and accomodative
reviews.text:
The property is huge and has many facilities like indoor pool, jacuzzi, gaming arena etc. However most other facilities were closed such as the boating in duck pond etc which would be open only in school holidays. 

The staff at the reception were to the book and refused to acknowledge my accor plus booking as I had booked through Agoda and wouldn’t even offer the welcome drink or even upgrade me to a better room in spite of availability also refused late checkout requests.  

We had paid a premium to buy breakfast and there were fruits, cornflakes, bread etc. But no hot breakfast options for vegetarians.  

There were no restaurants other than dominos who would deliver to the hotel. 

Property is good and big but ensure the facilities are open in case you are looking for a particular facility.
reviews.response:
Dear Arun,

Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re happy to hear that you enjoyed the property and its many facilities, including the indoor pool, jacuzzi, and gaming arena. We apologize for the closure of some activities, such as the boating at the duck pond, and appreciate your understanding that certain facilities are seasonal.

We’re truly sorry that your experience with our staff didn’t meet your expectations. We are committed to providing exceptional service, and your comments will be addressed with the team to ensure we are more welcoming and accommodating in the future.

Regarding the Accor Plus member benefits, please note that these are applicable only when booking directly with the resort or through the Accor website. As for late check-out requests, these are subject to availability, and unfortunately, we were unable to accommodate your request this time.

We also regret that the breakfast offerings did not fully meet your dietary needs. We will review the selection to p

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Clean, access to trails.
reviews.negatives: Captives, away from restaurants and the town. If you don’t have a car, good like finding uber or cabs.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
Check in was delay till 4pm. So many ppl were waiting for check in and the only coffee shop/lounge were full as it was small with limited seats. 

The play rooms/area are dirty and smells with food and garbage (not one day, but a few days we experience the same.)  The outdoor adventure area, pony riding are all closed. Nothing as described on the website. Disappointed.

reviews.title: The facilities/ kid's activities are the only saving grace. The rooms require significant refurb.
reviews.positives: The facilities and activities for the children is fantastic. The day spa was also amazing.
reviews.negatives:
For that price point the hotel was not comfortable and I would expect more: 
- there was very little water pressure in the showers which made it hard to shower and rinse my hair properly. The Facilities team tried changing the showerhead, but it made no difference. We ended up having to shower the kids down at the pool area where the water pressure was much better. We then needed to walk through reception and the hotel to get back to our room.
- the glass screen between the bath and the toilet was unable to be secured so there was a significant gap as it moved which left a lot of water all over the floor and an extremely soggy bathmat.
- the bed was hard and uncomfortable 
- the on-site restaurants booked out a week before our stay, so we travelled into town for food. The room service offerings are pretty average and not much variety. The menu fit all on one page.

reviews.title: Comfortable but needs improvement
reviews.text:
Room interior and lightings out-dated. Lights provided were too dim at night. 
Bathtub sealing not working at all thus unable to fill-up the tub :(
Not within walking distance to the nearest mall.

reviews.title: Comfortable and Clean, but nothing spectacularly "Fairmont" about it.
reviews.positives: Beds very comfy. Room was clean.
reviews.negatives: The food at Sublime Lounge was not good. Rock-hard pot pies, mouldy lettuce in Caesar Salad.

reviews.title: Mediocre
reviews.text:
This place was very average. Front desk staff were great and very helpful. Most on floor staff had great customer service skills. Kind and friendly.
Booked seafood buffet in one of their restaurants downstairs. No grilled fish, calamari. The hot food was cold. 
Spa was not in service(the reason i booked this hotel) and had a grime build up anyway.
Management, although kind and calm lacked any initiative to problem solve- it was like dealing with someone who was sleeping.
The place needs an upgrade. Its age is showing.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.positives: Definitely a family friendly hotel. Hotel rooms were very large. We were in a King Suite. The activities on offer for the kids were great, although not all were of interest to my daughter. There was one staff member at the lobby bar who made the most fantastic cocktails!!! And the lovely maintenance/gardener gave my car loving 2yo and I a quick trip on the ride on quad bike which was SO kind of him! The train ride was wonderful, we went twice!
reviews.negatives: Lack of cleaning/disinfecting, especially on the kids playgrounds and arcade rooms. The hand sanitisers were generally always empty and hard to find. The rooms were old, but that was expected and not as bad as I thought. The facilities and grounds were definitely tired. The pool towels were always out of stock when we tried to go to the pool.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Quite expensive for a simple overnight stay. Felt in need of a makeover. Carpark some distance from the entrance so would suggest offloading luggage at the entrance then parking. Nice location.

reviews.title: Our experience definitely fell flat of our expectations - has potential to be great.
reviews.positives:
Stayed for 3 nights with my 5yo. The location was fantastic. Short drive from all the blue mountain hot spots. I highly recommend the short drive to Sublime point lookout. It was breathtaking! Also scenic world is an absolute must. Blackheath markets were worth the short drive (even saw snow!) 

We were fully obsessed with the rainbow slide!!! Pricey- but worth it. (Only open on one day we were there out of 4) LOVED the ice skating as well! Again, very expensive for 90 min ($65) but we had a blast. Could’ve been user error on my part- but I thought the amenities like ice skating, movies, slide, train, arcade etc would be included in the price of the hotel. But you need to pay for almost everything (mirror maze and playground is free) the indoor playground is great for younger kids! 

Room service was great. Chicken Caesar salad was super tasty, as was the kids cheeseburger. And the fruit plate exceeded expectations! 

We loved the little shop front - expensive- but loved being able to get our sugar hit from the pick n mix lollies! Also great to buy gloves/hats as it was colder than expected!

Concierge was extremely helpful every time we needed them! Front desk were great too- you could tell it was v busy so I’m sure some things were missed. They were always soo friendly and smiley. overall a good experience checking in and out with front desk/concierge. I read a few reviews complaining about parking. We never had an issue and went during a long weekend. You can drop off your bags to concierge when checking out/in then walk to get your car. Not that dramatic. And the parking is free (thank god lol)

 I truly think this property has SUCH potential if they do a bit of a revamp, clean a bit more, and maybe lower the prices of the activities onsite. Wish I had more to say that was better- but a lot just fell flat.
reviews.negatives:
We checked into our room around 4pm and it was FREEZING. I’m talking -2 at night. I went to turn up the heater and it only goes to 26. We had to have a portable heater in our room the entire duration of the stay and it just didn’t heat properly.

Also, not sure if they put us in a pet friendly room (I’m very allergic to animals) but my asthma was out of control every time we spent longer than 20 min in the room. Very run down room as well - cracked sink, peeling wallpaper, dingy bath. Just wasn’t up to standard of how much we paid.

Beware- the walls are PAPER THIN! We could hear EVERYTHING. Our last night there - there was a party that went on til 3am. Loud music, shouting, banging - it was awful (on a Sunday night) You can literally hear a pin drop in the hallway.

The breakfast was a standard buffet breakfast- lots of options- however I found the “hot” breakfast items to be stone cold. Great that it’s open til 1030am.

I’ll say again- that the amenities they have showcased online were pricey. $2 per slide on the rainbow slide (both the train and merry go round weren’t operating the days we were there Fri-Mon) arcade games are a rip off- no chance in winning anything out of those machines - and $2 per game. Pretty standard price for arcade games but you don’t win tickets or anything so it’s just the entrainment. The mirror maze was awful - smelled horrible in there like vomit! The whole games area actually had such a strong awful smell. The indoor pool was extremely dirty - and a couple degrees colder than I would have liked during winter. You turn into an icy pole as soon as you get out. The spa was closed for maintenance whilst we were there - so can’t comment on that.

Overall- I had really high expectations of Fairmont as the pictures and reviews looked sooo promising. And I do believe they have an opportunity to be fantastic - but just didn’t quite hit the mark for us.

reviews.title: Hit and Miss - will not return
reviews.text:
Stayed there for 2 nights with my 2 young children. At first impression it was a nice hotel with great facilities and welcoming staff. However by the second day I found the hotel to be lacking in service and quality amongst other things.

I had ordered room service the second evening and shortly after ordering the hotel experienced a complete blackout (while my son was in the shower). We walked upstairs as the lifts and phones were not functional and stayed in the car to charge my phone and watch some movies as both my children were quite scared of being in the pitch black room (my daughter is only 20 months). While we were waiting the hotel lights came back on after approximately an hour so we started to return back to our room. Once we made it down the stairs to our floor we were greeted by the room service attendant who immediately asked upon seeing us what our room number was. When I told him he responded in a very rude and accusatory tone that he had been knocking on our door and waiting for us. It was as if we had inconvenienced him by leaving the premises and rather than apologising for the blackout he had the audacity to say we had made him wait!

When we returned to our room the power was still not working (ceiling lights were ok) so there was no tv or charging stations or internet to use. When I contacted reception they merely said that it was a rare occurrence, it was a local issue and not the fault of the hotel.

The following morning I received a note under the door from the hotel apologising for the inconvenience the blackout caused and that it was a local issue - the letter was seemingly left to ensure the guests were aware that it was not the caused by the hotel.

My son went to brush his teeth only to find there was no running water. I called reception and the phone rang out (this also happened the previous day when I ordered room service). I called again and after a long wait someone finally answered. I advised them we had no running water and they

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.positives: The school activities, the view and outdoor area.
reviews.negatives:
The room. Very old amenities, not cleaned properly. 
Old dusty carpet, old not fully functional shower head and tap. The rooms were not vacuumed, cleaned or tidied every day. 
Breakfast was decent.

reviews.title: Tired hotel that could be much better
reviews.text: We really looked forward to staying at this 'Fairmont' hotel having stayed at other hotels in this brand. We were quite disappointed. While the weather wasn't great and that is certainly not the fault of the hotel, we had hoped we would be staying at a top quality establishment. Sadly this was not the case. The hotel was opened nearly 30 years ago and seems to have been devoid of maintenance in the intervening years. The bathroom was small and dark with very poor lighting but there was sufficient to see the mould on the tiles above the bath. The TV was susceptible to cutting out because the cabling to it had poor connections. Indeed we had to call their maintenance man out who didn't really seem surprised by this fact.  The woodwork around our room window frame and the exterior woodwork in the public area had so much paint peeling that it was mostly bare and starting to rot. The carpets in the room were stained in several places. The doors on the elevator were faulty to the extent that when our baggage trolley was exiting they partially stuck for a few minutes. Service in the hallways was not much better. When we went to breakfast, the person in a nearby room had placed their room service trays outside for collection. They were still there in late afternoon - about 6 hours later. There main restaurant was closed in the evening leaving only their coffee shop eating area open.  Not what you expect for a high end hotel which charges top dollar.  We ended up going into the local village (and having a great dinner) but the hotel should have had a better on-site dining option. In the hallway near the swimming pool there was clearly several leaks in the ceiling as they had placed buckets and towels on the floor to catch the drips of water.  There's so much more wrong with this hotel but I probably don't need to provide any further examples.

reviews.title: Expensive without extras you would expect
reviews.positives: Great big room
reviews.negatives:
Quilt on bed far too hot. Why is this done when so easy to use blankets or a light quilt 

Also no biscuits with yea and coffee. An expensive stay with limited consideration for comfort

reviews.title: not value for money
reviews.text: The room and water kettle are not clean.  The shower is not of good water pressure.
reviews.positives: nice environment

reviews.title: Good facility for families but not everything available mid week in school holidays
reviews.positives: The breakfast was good
reviews.negatives: The room was sooooo noisy from inconsiderate people upstairs :( I called multiple times with little help to the situation. Also school holiday activities were finished before the last 2 weeks of holidays! Didn’t make sense at all with so many families there and seeing the activities but not being able to use them on non weekends.

reviews.title: restaurant staff service was extremely poor.
reviews.positives: size of room, location, and garden

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.positives: Great location in the heart of Blue mountains
reviews.negatives: Outdated furniture, former glory of a once great hotel. Canteen food, nothing other than tea in the room, not even a bottle of water.

reviews.title: Service with a smile.. but no service!
reviews.text:
This hotel has so much potential... but just hasn't got there yet. Arriving at 3.30pm we were informed that our room wasn't ready yet... so a free drink on the house and an hour later we were still waiting. After chasing the bar staff for an update we went down to our room on level two. Located just near the pool the room was a good size and the bed comfortable, however the pillows are awful. The bathroom is small and the shower terrible pressure wise, and housekeeping could probably spend a little longer in there. On our second day we returned to the room to find the mini bar attendant reading our newspaper from the couch area, not sure who was more surprised... We were very confused with the restaurant and bar opening hours, the coffee lounge didnt open, and the Eucalypt restaurant was open outside of normal advertised hours - very confusing and we only realised when trying to leave to patron another restaurant in leura. Staff are friendly but so disorganised, the bar area is a nightmare, and there appears to be no cohesion between staff. Breakfast is a disaster, extremely busy, not enough staff and impossible to get new cutlery etc when using a table previously occupied. We tried the pizza from the bar on the second night, it was undercooked and pretty average, and the trio of dips somehow got downgraded to a duo...?
The hotel is being renovated, but it all seems rather slap dash. The exteriors need a good sweep and the cobwebs inside and out are not appealing.
reviews.positives: Location

reviews.title: Great fun for small children.
reviews.positives: Great activities for little kids. Beautiful location.
reviews.negatives: Very dated interior. The blockout curtains had holes in them. The kids play area had a terrible stale smell.

reviews.title: Noise. No for light sleeper
reviews.text:
Too noisy.

Woke up multiple times due to the noise from corridor and from window (during early hours when kid is playing on the playground).

Not complaint about the people who cause the noise. Their 'noise' is not unreasonable

reviews.title: Great kids activities onsite...but stay elsewhere.
reviews.positives: The kids' activities were varied and entertaining. We especially liked that ice-skating was onsite.
reviews.negatives: The hotel is in desperate need of a renovation. It is clearly a family-focused hotel, but the rooms look corporate, and the carpets are threadbare (not really conducive to kids on the floor). The bathroom was missing most of the grout and we even had a tile come away from the wall during the stay.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Rooms dated, most staff are friendly

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.positives: great location helpful staff
reviews.negatives: poor room sound isolation ,we could easily hear our next door room smaller moves the facility is quite old and the amount of money you pay does not match the service you receive

reviews.title: Dated and unclean. Please renovate!
reviews.text:
Been coming here since the 90’s. Used to be great but unfortunately needs a good renovation in the rooms and amenities, to bring back the Fairmont luxury and magic!
Carpets need replacing and restaurants revamped. Nostalgia is the only thing going for it at the moment.
Would love the see it come back with a fresh reopening.

reviews.title: It was good location peaceful but we have been put in the ground floor where you cna hear early and nights all babies na
reviews.positives: Location

reviews.title: Can do better than this
reviews.text:
It's our 3rd time returning with my wife and kid as we were recommending good facilities & atmosphere for family and kids.
But this time, we had a bad experience. 1st of all, water in both swimming pool & whirlpool is obviously dirtier than last 2 visits. We always came during school holidays, there shouldn't be any excuses for maintenance of all facilities, specially the water. My boy 5-year-old boy felt belly pain and threw up twice on the night of 15/7, and caught high fever starting around 12pm.  As it got worse, I walked to Reception myself around 1pm for first-aid assist, and they explained no Panadol and opened the Kiosk shop also found no any related medicine for under 7-year-old, also cooling patch is not available. Then I had to drive all the way out to 24hrs petrol store for Panadol after 1am midnight. I meant as family-choice hotel, specially during school holidays, it's not hard for the management to predict there are a lot of kids inhouse, those common medicines should be available in case of any emergency use. Front Office and Manger on Duty should be very clear abt this and provide assist right away.
Indoor swimming pool toilet & swimming /whirlpool should be paid much more attention as it's absolutely the hottest spot during winter holidays. Towels were running out from 16/7 5:20pm not replenished more than 45 mins, a lot of guests were complaining abt that.
Moreover, room floor corridor was quite dirty.
Other than these, the new Jumping castle was told 2 tickets for 10mins at the Concierge expanding desk while checked in (15/7), however, it became 5mins only when played (honestly, it's quite bad in value of money). 
Lastly, staffs are generally still friendly and nice, movies are well organized, Magic Craig is still doing a good show, kids like him.

reviews.title: Enjoyable golf group getaway.
reviews.positives: Staff were excellent and helpful. Dinner at Embers was very good. Rooms were comfortable. The hotel is a little dated.
reviews.negatives: The golf course and club house facilities were below the standard we were expecting.

reviews.title: Best for families, not couples.
reviews.text: Great for families, bad for a quiet retreat.  Very kid friendly and expect lots of yelling, screaming, and running about with overwhelmed staff unable to do anything about it.  Food was great for all meals eaten on site, but again paying big bucks for dinner while kids running in the dining room.  Property a bit over work and under maintained in areas.  Staff was great but absolutely overwhelmed and exhausted.  Rooms okay, but needing some attention.  Surprised to find the Fairmont brand associated with this property since Fairmont has always stood for excellence.
reviews.response:

We will certainly review our approach to maintaining a quieter environment in public areas for those seeking a more serene experience.

reviews.room_type: Deluxe King Room with Spa Bath

reviews.title: Acceptable.
reviews.positives: The early check-in was very efficient and easy and the room was comfortable.
reviews.negatives: I booked for dinner on Sunday night for 7.00pm. in the Fairmont's restaurant We arrived at approx 6.55pm and were shown to our table. We were immediately provided with water but then had to wait, UNSUCCESSFULLY for in excess of 20 minutes for any further attention from the wait staff, despite making eye contact with the waiter who had shown us to our table. Also, it appeared that tables that were seated after us were giving their drink/food orders while we bided our time. This was totally unacceptable to US, so we left. It certainly coloured our opinion of The Fairmont.

reviews.title: 1 star for cleanliness in 5star facilities
reviews.text:
I read many review saying the room is dirty, so I’m a bit worry. Prior to check in I request for extra cleaning since my family have allergies.
However is so hard to get the confirmation if my request can be done. I tried email and call few times to follow up and only got reply on my check in day saying they will extra dust the room. However when I arrived, there is food stain and crumbs in the carpet, both bedside table are so dusty, in the bathtub, towel and bathroom floor have hair. No slipper and do not disturb sign in the room. The table was so wobbly and is a hazard.  The shower pressure was low (I already knew from the review that I read prior), but the hand wash water pressure is high and splash everywhere. The breakfast was exactly the same in 2 consecutive day. Usually 4/5 star hotel have 1/2 item to rotate.
However, to be fair, the kids facilities was good and that the only reason we stay there. 
Is such a shame a nice resort with good facilities lack of the room cleanliness, which is essential and basic standard in hotel.
reviews.response:

Thank you for sharing your detailed feedback with us. We deeply regret that your stay did not meet the high standards we strive for, particularly in terms of cleanliness, and we truly apologize for the issues you encountered during your visit.

We sincerely apologize for the food stains, dust, and hair you found in the room, as well as the other maintenance issues like the wobbly table and low shower pressure. This certainly does not reflect the quality we aim to deliver, and we will address these concerns with both our housekeeping and maintenance teams to ensure they are rectified.

Regarding the breakfast, we understand your disappointment with the lack of variety, and we appreciate your feedback. We strive to offer a rotating menu to cater to all tastes, and we will review this to ensu

reviews.title: Great location, fun for kids but certainly needs a renovation!
reviews.positives: Lovely location and so many kids activities.
reviews.negatives: Pool was cold and couldn’t be enjoyed. Gym wasn’t clean, peloton bike didn’t connect, rooms need update and more detailed cleans.

reviews.title: Living off its Name
reviews.text: A nice building but very pricey and not worth the money
reviews.response:
Dear Peter,

We very much appreciate your comments regarding your stay at the resort.

It was very pleasing to see there were aspects of your stay that were enjoyable. I am sorry however that some areas did not meet your expectations. 

Thank you again for sharing your experience, and we hope to welcome you back to the resort again very soon.

General Manager

reviews.title: Overall ... OK. Hotel is positioned for families ... please renovate worn timber / carpets.
reviews.positives: Overall experience
reviews.negatives:
Rooms and hotel foyer look "tired" e.g. scuffed timber work around entry; mis-shaped and worn carpets in Room 102.

Children riding their bikes around the foyer and corridors.

reviews.title: Disappointing
reviews.text: Resort was at capacity and very busy with young families. Our rooms were located on the second floor just off the lobby next to Kidzone and pool -  extremely noisy with kids and parents consistently yelling and running down the corridor. Rooms were tired and housekeeping service was only available on request - this wasn’t advised at check-in and we only discovered it when we returned to our rooms in the afternoon to find notes under our doors. We were really excited for a weekend away at this resort but were disappointed with our experience.

reviews.title: Lovely resort, good location among the Blue Mountains
reviews.positives: Location was ideal for us.  The views when you walk into the lobby was stunning.
reviews.negatives: The whiskey bar opening hours were limited. The Sublime Lounge menu options were limited along with room service, ideally for a facility of the size there would be a better selection.  The resort had a lovely relaxed feel but could do with some TLC  but wouldn't stop us from staying again.

reviews.title: Indifferent
reviews.text:
I usually stay here every few months with my daughter and am usually very pleased with the hotel. I was dissapointed this time there were no tissues in the hotel or bottle opener in room. I was told I would need to call every time I wanted a bottle open as they only had one opener in the whole hotel, bottles needing opening should have not been in the mini bar. The bed sheet was ripped. 
The concierge and desk staff as usual were very helpful.
reviews.response:
Dear Fran
Firstly thank you for your ongoing loyalty to the Fairmont Resort & Spa - it is very much valued and appreciated by us all here.
I am pleased our concierge and front desk reception staff were very helpful throughout your stay.
I was extremely disappointed to read of your experience with both the bed linen and the bottle opener. This is, as you would know from previous experience, highly unlike our usual product and service levels. I wanted to assure you your comments have been discussed with our House Keeping manager for immediate review with her team as it is not acceptable. Thank you so much for taking the time to bring this to our attention, we certainly appreciate the opportunity to immediately rectify these issues.
Fran, we look forward to continuing to welcome you back in the future and are confident your normal service levels will be experienced once again.

Kind regards

Scott O'Neile
General Manager

reviews.title: Enjoyable - never been to the Blue Mountains before

reviews.negatives: Golf Club lunch was very disappointing - staff didnt seem interested about ant feedback

reviews.title: Old and dated room
reviews.text:
Peaceful nice setting but out of the way,
Only one restaurant open for dinner  during the week , hot spa not open either , disappointed  . Swimming pools would be nice in summer. If your not paying golf then mabe look for a different location that has more restaurants and things to do.

reviews.title: Overall a good stay.
reviews.positives: Great location and facilities
reviews.negatives: Staff seemed like they were forced into the job and not enjoying it one bit, except for the Arab concierge guy and Indonesian lady at the restaurant.

reviews.title: Not worth it
reviews.text: Pricey, poor breakfast spread, extras like Wifi connection and bottled water were chargeable at exorbitant rates.

reviews.title: 60th Birthday Surprise
reviews.negatives: Embers restaurant. Seated in the Jamison section. Cold, clinical and noisy. I requested a table of 7 in Embers but was told they don’t do this. Upon arrival saw a large group seated there. Wouldn’t book there again.

reviews.title: good hotel good location
reviews.text: It was the second time we family spent 2 nights there. It is quite and tidy. Near the Leura town and Three Sisters Atrractions
reviews.positives: location, environment, quite

reviews.negatives: The property looks very dated and tired. Hard to justify the price considering the condition of the property

reviews.title: Generally comfortable.
reviews.text: Generally Good but Hotel still lacking 85 some areas.  No assistance with bags, long corridors to carry bags to lifts. Amenities like toiletries & fresh milk or free water not available in rooms. Standard rooms very basic & still needing upgrade. Hotel needs more general amenities for guests. Piano or music in bar, food at bar & shops etc
reviews.positives: Nice location.  Scenic views. Close to shops

reviews.title: Average
reviews.positives: Restaurant and view
reviews.negatives:
The experience was okay overall, but I don’t think it’s worth coming here just for the holiday activities. The ticket prices have doubled in the past six months, which makes it feel less affordable.

Jumping Castle: This was a bit disappointing. It’s $6 for 5 minutes of play, and they time it with a stopwatch. It’s also quite high and steep, which scared my kid after just one try. It would’ve been nice if they mentioned the time limit when selling the tickets.

Rainbow Slide: Much better. At $2 per slide, it’s still fun and relatively reasonable, even though it’s pricier than before. My kid really enjoyed this one.

Bathrooms: Not very convenient for kids. There’s no handheld showerhead, and you have to step into a bathtub to shower, which makes it tricky for little ones.

Overall, while there were some enjoyable parts, the price increases and lack of family-friendly features make it less appealing for a holiday outing.

reviews.title: Great Resort
reviews.text:
Hotel room is fair, but clean & tidy.

Location is a bit far away from city centre and difficult to find nearby resturants.
reviews.positives: Quiet & Peace

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: Pet friendly room
reviews.negatives: Lack of noise insulation at our front door. We frequently heard noise at all hours of the night and early morning which disrupted our sleep and our dog

reviews.title: Not recommended for couples
reviews.text: More than a resort & spa  I would describe this place as a summer camp for families. The place is designed for children’s entertainment and although the facilities are nice they are dirty and unorganised. Staff seemed to be overwhelmed with the excess of guests and young kids running wildly all over the place so it was hard to find tables in the restaurant or someone to clean the tables once you found one. I had to clear  and wipe the table my self as after asking at least 3 staff no one went there to help, even the guy who brought our food to the table left the dirty dishes from the previous guest there. The buffet breakfast felt like being in a 5 years old birthday where no parents were looking after the kids. The pool area was the Wild West of children, and smell like urine to say the least. The rooms had thin walls so you could hear the fights between children’s and the parents arguing when the voices raised and seems that on holidays no one had an early bedtime. Very disappointed about this place. Only recommended for parents o people who love kids and noise.
reviews.response:
